name: Repair the A-side lookahead as an explicit fixed-shape row-pair preload, then revalidate correctness

summary.name: Repair the A-side lookahead as an explicit fixed-shape row-pair preload, then revalidate correctness
summary.hypothesis: This run is not acceptable as-is because correctness failed on all three cases, but it is still informative: runtime improved from 30.592960 ms to 30.270464 ms and the hot-band kernel shortened from about 41.19 us to about 40.99 us. That combination suggests the A-side Ps2r idea may be directionally right while the recursive row-pair implementation is wrong. The best next move is therefore not to throw the family away immediately, but to rewrite the row-pair lookahead into a flatter fixed-shape sequence that preloads the second row-pair explicitly, removes the recursive fragment handoff, and checks whether the performance signal survives once correctness is restored.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Implementation / codegen risk in the current A-side lookahead path, plus residual shared-to-register A-feed latency if the idea survives repair.
summary.risk: Moderate. The rewrite is local and directly motivated by a real speed signal, but if correctness is restored only by undoing the additional overlap, the family may collapse back to the round-2 baseline.
summary.stop_condition: Reject the A-side lookahead family if the flatter rewrite still fails correctness or if restoring correctness erases the hot-band/runtime improvement completely.

notes: Node C may now implement this one direction.
